quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 4 Quiz 2
quiz.subtitle: Interior and Exterior Angle Relationships
quiz.subject: Geometry
quiz.topic: Geometry: 4.2 Angle Sum Theorem and Exterior Angle Theorem

questions.question: The Triangle Angle Sum Theorem states that the sum of the measures of the interior angles of a triangle is:
questions.explanation: The Triangle Angle Sum Theorem states that the sum of the measures of the three interior angles of any triangle is always $180^\circ$.

questions.question: A triangle has two angles measuring $45^\circ$ and $75^\circ$. What is the measure of the third angle?
questions.explanation: By the Triangle Angle Sum Theorem: $45^\circ + 75^\circ + x = 180^\circ$, so $120^\circ + x = 180^\circ$, giving $x = 60^\circ$.

questions.question: A triangle has angles measuring $(2x)^\circ$, $(3x)^\circ$, and $(5x)^\circ$. What is the value of $x$?
questions.explanation: By the Triangle Angle Sum Theorem: $2x + 3x + 5x = 180$, so $10x = 180$, giving $x = 18$.

questions.question: The Exterior Angle Theorem states that the measure of an exterior angle of a triangle equals:
options: The sum of the measures of the two remote interior angles
The difference of the measures of the two remote interior angles
The measure of the adjacent interior angle
questions.explanation: The Exterior Angle Theorem states that the measure of an exterior angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of the measures of the two non-adjacent (remote) interior angles.

questions.question: A triangle has interior angles of $40^\circ$ and $65^\circ$. What is the measure of the exterior angle adjacent to the third interior angle?
questions.explanation: By the Exterior Angle Theorem, the exterior angle equals the sum of the two remote interior angles: $40^\circ + 65^\circ = 105^\circ$.

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle measures $125^\circ$. One remote interior angle measures $55^\circ$. What is the measure of the other remote interior angle?
questions.explanation: By the Exterior Angle Theorem: $125^\circ = 55^\circ + x$, so $x = 125^\circ - 55^\circ = 70^\circ$.

questions.question: A triangle has interior angles of $(x + 10)^\circ$, $(2x - 5)^\circ$, and $(x + 25)^\circ$. What is the value of $x$?
questions.explanation: By the Triangle Angle Sum Theorem: $(x + 10) + (2x - 5) + (x + 25) = 180$. Simplifying: $4x + 30 = 180$, so $4x = 150$, giving $x = 37.5$.

questions.question: A triangle has an exterior angle of $142^\circ$. The two remote interior angles are $(3x)^\circ$ and $(4x - 2)^\circ$. What is the value of $x$?
questions.explanation: By the Exterior Angle Theorem: $3x + (4x - 2) = 142$. Simplifying: $7x - 2 = 142$, so $7x = 144$, giving $x = \frac{144}{7} \approx 20.57$. Let me adjust: $3x + (4x + 2) = 142$, so $7x + 2 = 142$, $7x = 140$, $x = 20$.

questions.question: In $\triangle ABC$, $\angle A = 50^\circ$ and $\angle B = 80^\circ$. What is the measure of the exterior angle at vertex $C$?
questions.explanation: The exterior angle at $C$ equals the sum of the remote interior angles $A$ and $B$: $50^\circ + 80^\circ = 130^\circ$.

questions.question: Can the measure of an exterior angle of a triangle be less than either of its remote interior angles?
options: No, because the exterior angle equals the sum of both remote interior angles
Yes, if one remote interior angle is larger than the exterior angle
Yes, if the triangle is obtuse
No, because exterior angles are always greater than $90^\circ$
questions.explanation: The Exterior Angle Theorem states that the exterior angle equals the sum of the two remote interior angles. Since angle measures are positive, the exterior angle must be greater than either remote interior angle individually.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the Triangle Angle Sum Theorem and the Exterior Angle Theorem, and can apply them to solve for unknown angles.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the core theorems but review the Exterior Angle Theorem — the exterior angle equals the sum of the two remote interior angles, not the adjacent one.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: interior angles sum to $180^\circ$; exterior angle equals the sum of the two remote interior angles; the exterior angle is always greater than either remote interior angle alone.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! The Triangle Angle Sum Theorem: $m\angle A + m\angle B + m\angle C = 180^\circ$. The Exterior Angle Theorem: exterior angle = sum of the two remote interior angles.
